page: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores based on the Intel Xeon 8124M processor with an x86_64 architecture running at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the c5n.18xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, scoring 66660.83 on stress-ng and 49891.05 on the Passmark CPU test. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with cached read-only speeds reaching 3704.68 GB/sec and uncached large-block write speeds of 90.55 GB/sec. Database operations also perform strongly, registering 22220.88 on Passmark. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are relatively average or weak, with the 7B model text generation reaching only 7.23 tokens per second.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it highly suitable for network-bound workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory bandwidth deliver high resource density for parallel processing without the overhead of local storage maintenance. This profile is well-suited for high-performance computing, network appliances, distributed database operations, and multi-threaded batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs and 36 physical Intel Xeon 8124M cores operating at 3.4 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, offering 2.67 GB per core, and lacks local storage or GPU hardware. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. In benchmark testing, it exhibits strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, though single-core execution and LLM text generation remain average to weak. This hardware profile is highly efficient for network-intensive applications, multi-threaded batch processing, and distributed database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 72 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-bandwidth, compute-optimized platform featuring seventy-two virtual cores and one hundred gigabits per second of baseline network throughput.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing high memory bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for network-bound applications
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for distributed databases, high-performance computing, and batch processing
